title: lmkit: fleet ops CLI (status / start / stop) in Go or Rust
description:
A real lmkit CLI so runs are usable WITHOUT an agent: see and control workers without ssh+systemctl+tail. Today lmkit/cli.py (Python) has only 'quickstart'; the operational layer (launching runs, systemd units, run_frontier.sh, monitoring) is all one-off scaffolding outside lmkit. Want a single binary: 'lmkit status' (workers across boxes: project/run, step, loss, tok/s, alive/last-seen, GPU), 'lmkit start/stop <worker>', and ideally 'lmkit run' to create the durable systemd --user unit so launching is one command not a remembered incantation.

## Context
Guy 2026-06-19: 'does lmkit have any status commands? ... start/stop as needed.' + 'I want to make it easy for me to use when I don't have an agent running.' + 'the cli will be in rust or go. not python.'
design: Go or Rust (per Guy's new-tools rule [[go-for-data-llm-tools]]), NOT Python — the torch training core stays Python in lmkit, the ops/CLI layer is a compiled binary. status reads the same source as the Workers dashboard (metrics.jsonl tail per run + systemd unit state), or queries each box's lmkit-metrics exporter :9837. start/stop wraps systemctl --user. Cross-box via ssh per configured box. Absorbs the current one-offs (run_frontier.sh orchestration, ad-hoc systemd units) into versioned subcommands. Overlaps the training-scripts consolidation.

title: lmkit: emit Prometheus metrics natively
description:
lmkit workers emit Prometheus metrics directly (step/loss/lr/tok_per_sec/val_loss/peak_vram/params/max_steps + last-update ts) so every worker is scrapeable without a side exporter reading metrics.jsonl. The metrics.jsonl->exporter dashboard (in the training repo, deploy/monitoring/lmkit_metrics_exporter.py) is the stopgap to retire once this lands.

## Context
Guy 2026-06-19: 'right now just wire up metrics.jsonl. make a bead for lmkit prom.' + 'put the generic lmkit relevant bead in that repo.'
design: Add a prom sink in lmkit/observability.py mirroring make_run (the Aim hook): opt-in via env, no-op + never fail the run if unset/unwritable. Preferred mechanism: node_exporter textfile collector — write <LMKIT_PROM_TEXTFILE_DIR>/lmkit_<run>.prom atomically (tmp+rename) on each log_metrics call, labels {project,run,host,device}; node_exporter (:9100/box, already scraped) serves it. Wire into pretrain.run + sft (shared log_metrics path). Cloud/NAT upgrade path: a Pushgateway sink on the same hook. The Python side just writes the .prom file — no new daemon.
